h2h_summary: The Netherlands are unbeaten in all three previous meetings with Japan, winning two and drawing one, including a 1-0 victory in the 2010 FIFA World Cup group stage.
team_style_home: Netherlands under Ronald Koeman deploy a possession-based 4-2-3-1 that transitions into a 4-2-5 in build-up, emphasizing central midfield control through De Jong and Gravenberch, with wingers cutting inside to create overloads and fullbacks providing width.
team_style_away: Japan under Hajime Moriyasu use a flexible 3-4-2-1 / 4-2-3-1 system featuring high-intensity pressing, compact defensive blocks against stronger opponents, and rapid vertical transitions with technically gifted wide forwards exploiting space in behind.
wc_history_home: The Netherlands are one of football's most storied underachievers, having reached three World Cup finals (1974, 1978, 2010) without winning, and have been a consistent contender in every tournament they have qualified for.
wc_history_away: Japan have qualified for every World Cup since 1998 and reached their peak in 2022 by sensationally defeating Germany and Spain to top their group before being eliminated by Croatia on penalties.
group_context: Group F consists of Netherlands, Japan, Sweden, and Tunisia, with the top two teams advancing; Netherlands enter as heavy favourites to top the group, while Japan — full of confidence after pre-tournament wins over Scotland, England, and Iceland — are widely regarded as the main challengers for second place.
strengths_home: World-class defensive spine anchored by Virgil van Dijk, providing commanding aerial and positional dominance
Creative midfield depth with De Jong, Simons, and Gravenberch capable of controlling and unlocking compact defences
Prolific attacking options including Gakpo and depth across the frontline with Depay, Malen, and Brobbey
strengths_away: Exceptional recent form with five consecutive wins including scalps against England and Scotland in pre-tournament friendlies
Organised high-press system that has dismantled elite European opposition through collective pressing and quick transitions
Experienced captain Wataru Endo provides tactical discipline and leadership, with technically gifted forwards Kubo and Doan as constant threats
